steps: Put chopped banana in a closed ziploc-like bag
Add diluted 1:10 dish soap solution and 1g table salt to fruits
Mash it until homogeneous mixture is obtained
Strain the mixture through a household coffee filter
Add 25 ml isopropyl alcohol to strained liquid
Let it sit for 5 min until phase separation
Collect upper white layer (precipitated DNA) with a toothpick, place it on a new coffee filter and wash it with 70% ethanol
Pat it dry with paper towels
Dissolve DNA in 1 ml TE buffer
Set 1 μl aside; store rest at 4°C
Prepare the rehydration solution for the RPA %s %s
Rehydrate 1 RPA reaction pellet in each one of the rehydration solutions
Pipette up and down to resuspend pellet
Heat the reaction at 39°C for 20 minutes.
Run a gel electrophoresis: %s %s Lane123SampleDNA ladderSamplePCR control
should do a standard DNA purif kit (which we don't have at the moment) so we'll try the same purif as we did for the banana DNA
